notes: Spec asserts: ready → working → ready and state never enters waiting. The recipe drives a single auto-executed bash call ('echo hello') followed by a terminal reply. Vibe is a single-birth adapter (session id = the session_<ts>_<hash> dir via SessionIDFromPath); session_birth is anchored to 'start' UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession or the real session (whichever the process scanner mints first) satisfies the birth, and the turn phases anchor forward onto the real session. Under bypass_tool_permissions the bash tool auto-runs (no permission dialog), so PermissionPending is never set and waiting is structurally unreachable — the assistant(tool_calls)→tool_result cycle is just continued working activity, and the text-only terminal assistant (turn_done) is the only ready settle. If the recording ever flips to waiting, the bash tool tripped a gate (different feature) and the recipe/settings must be tightened. observations: agent=mistral-vibe; model=mistral-medium-3.5 (confirmed live meta.json config.active_model default, passes NormalizeModelName unchanged); total_tokens_nonzero=true (turn_done emits sidecar stats.context_tokens into Tokens.Total → summary.total_tokens); tokens_nonzero is deliberately NOT asserted (vibe never populates cum input/output usage, only the context total); cost_nonzero=false (the adapter reads no cost field); context_window/utilization not asserted (mistral-medium-3.5 absent from the capacity table).

text: Session appears in ready at launch (pre-session proc-<PID> or the session_<ts>_<hash> id, whichever arrives first)
User prompt drives ready → working (spec bullet 1)
Turn completes back to ready; the bash tool_call ran auto-executed (bypass_tool_permissions) and state never entered waiting (spec bullet 2); final state ready
